Entry13274 ASAN NAPUNTA ANG PERA UB? I know nakakasawa na pakinggan yung rant naming mga graduating high school students, [REDACTED_NAME] question lang — intrams [REDACTED_NAME] event naging one day... tapos ₱150,000 ang budget? Ate, asan? “Credible people in position” already confirmed na may ₱150k na hawak for intrams alone, hindi pa kasama yung ibang budget ng school mismo na sila maghahandle. And don’t get me wrong — [REDACTED_NAME] for events. We want good programs. We want proper equipment, maayos na venues, prizes na pinag-isipan. [REDACTED_NAME], [REDACTED_NAME] kalaking budget? Hindi ito automatic accusation [REDACTED_NAME] corruption. This is students asking for transparency. [REDACTED_NAME] breakdown, walang explanation, [REDACTED_NAME]. A simple expense report would clear everything up — magkano sa logistics, facilities, prizes, staffing — [REDACTED_NAME]. Intrams are supposed to build school spirit, memories, and community. [REDACTED_NAME] instead na excitement, ang naiiwan sa students ay confusion at duda. Transparency doesn’t weaken leadership — it strengthens trust. And trust is something [REDACTED_NAME] pa kaysa sa anumang event. But hell no, lets assume 200k, for 1 day? When we were in grade 10, prior to the tuition increase, we had so many activities. Career day, UN day, even a concert, and a 1 week long intramurals. Fast forward 2 years, and now we’ll have a 1 day intramurals that btw we had to fight for... (Im a secretary and nung meeting namin, she attacked US as if we posted those other submissions, not that we dont agree with them) We’re about to graduate, but we still care about this school and the students who’ll stay after us. [REDACTED_NAME] nagtatanong. Not to attack, but to hold systems to the same standards of accountability na tinuturo [REDACTED_NAME]. Dont attack us for fighting, we’re thought this is the highest for of learning. (Also I read some of you na este we weren’t forced to enroll in UB, but yes we choose UB cuz they were great in my prior years yet now? no more silence. Ub is the true definition of advertisement vs reality. Swimming pool nga nasa website nila pero prank [REDACTED_NAME].) If ₱150,000 can disappear into one rushed day without answers, then the real event we’re waiting for isn’t intrams — it’s transparency finally showing up. After the event magulat nlng naka macbook na si ateng nasa [REDACTED_NAME].

Entry13222 Don't do wife stuff for a boyfriend Shoutout sa ex [REDACTED_NAME] senior high—I hope you get to read this someday. When your dad was struggling to pay your tuition, I didn’t hesitate about helping because I wanted to see you graduate, even if it was just senior high. When you had so many bills to pay, I always prepare baon for us, and every time pupunta ka dito sa bahay, I’d prepare something for you para hndi ka na kakain sainyo. [REDACTED_NAME] and i didn't complain. On our anniversary, I was the one who provided clothes for you so you'd look presentable naman because you didn’t have proper clothes. I bought the cake, gave you money for a haircut, and I didn’t complain. Tinutulungan pa kita lagi sa mga school activities tas binagsak mo 4sub. Every date we had, I was always the one paying—never 50/50. I understood we were both students at the time, and I wasn’t expecting you to pay all the time, but why couldn’t you even treat me somewhere simple? As long as I am with you, I'm happy [REDACTED_NAME] e—but I didn’t see any effort from you. On our last date, I planned it, I paid for our transportation papunta at pauwi, I paid for our dinner, and you even ordered two plates. It wasn’t in my budget, but I still chose to buy it for you. I didn’t know that would be our last date before you decided to break up with me, right before graduation. You said, “We’re not growing anymore,” even though you knew I was trying every single day to fight for our relationship because I already saw my future with you. But you still chose to leave. Honestly, I feel like you left because you couldn’t pay your debt. You didn’t have to leave me—we could have worked things out. But now, you’re ignoring all my messages about the money you borrowed. I still wonder... why did you leave so suddenly? Why were you already with someone else while I was still here, trying to understand if you ever loved me or if you just used me because you benefited from me. So to everyone reading this—Don’t do husband or wife things for someone who’s just your boyfriend or girlfriend.

Entry13218 How to ACTUALLY spot AI, a guide for idi*ts, student leaders, and professors Article sana ‘to eh, but I’m too f*ck**g frustrated so buckle your seatbelts. We have completely normalized AI in LEARNING spaces, tapos tayo pa talaga ung may audacity [REDACTED_NAME] bakit kinulang tayo sa media literacy. I am so f**k*ng sick of struggling to catch up with my own groupmates just because I absolutely refuse to use Chatgpt, while they frolic in their little fields of delusion. WARNING: I will not coddle your egos. NOTE: Recognizing AI does not mean deeming a work “too perfect”, rather, it’s about using human empathy to resonate with a piece of work. Here’s my little list of tells, please feel free to add more 1. Bullet-form answers. This especially applies to AI’d research and reports, and you’ll recognize the same goddamn format the first prompt. Even if you prompt the AI to reformat it into an essay, you will see those concisely written sentences if you Actually read. You will know what I’m talking about if you’re an avid reader of classics, or if you engage with general media meaningfully. NO HUMAN is ever that concise no matter how passionate or precise they try to be! I know pastors, lawyers, government workers, journalists, even people who are masters in theology who are so incapable of giving the same concision that AI has, no matter how many times the paper’ been edited and rewritten. Humans, from the dawn of f*ck**g time, have always had some kind of special relationship with every information they absorb, with their own special way of communicating them. Have you seriously not noticed how every single group report seems to have the same format? Same flow of information? 2. Clinical language. Soulless bast**ds. This s**t should be obvious, but it’s so normalized because it’s difficult to argue against, or professors/leaders don’t want to bother. It’s so OBVIOUS! It is impersonal! It’s so disconnected from the author! There is an obvious barrier between author and work and we are being deliberately ignorant to it. Even legal work isn’t that f**k**g disconnected from the author. Nothing is ever actually objective. 3. No tone or voice. Don’t even get me f*ck**g started. Even formal language has a voice to it. There is intention and deliberation. Formal language is capable of pushing even the most subtle f*ck**g agendas. People use this all the time to plant little seeds of ideas into minds of others. I urge you to relearn how to empathize with people and reconnect with your own sense of humanity, and you Will see it reflect in any written work. 4. Ai cannot f*ck**g write narrative analyses. Analysis requires interpretation, which requires careful decision-making, which is inherently subjective. If you’ve ever asked it to write your paper for LRIZAL, it most likely produced a soulless piece of tr**h. Do not f*ck**g insult our writers by using AI to analyze their masterful writing. Languages are tools of communication and they shape the way you feel and hope and express your self, even in the most formal of settings. In ten years’ time, the use of AI will be the very thing that we will STRUGGLE to unlearn from our f**k*ng psyches. Back then, I would giggle at 3 am as I try to sneakily reference a meme for a paper. You have the f**k*ng AUDACITY to tell me that you spent all f**ki*g night to write a paper when all you’ve done is fat-finger your f**k*ng keyboards and let AI do everything? Don’t even bother throwing your little “AI is a tool 🥺” argument at me. Don’t bother telling me that you “curate” the answers you get from AI. You f*ck**g ILL**ERAT*S. You are being CODDLED. You have zero f***ing integrity. You have sold your soul to the de**l. Your dign**y was lost the moment you tapped on that as***le lookin ahh AI logo, and you will be burying it deeper into H**L if you try to even defend it. You have allowed AI to steal away your own perseverance and I am personally grieving it because YOU wouldn’t. If me shaming you away from using AI is what it takes for you [REDACTED_NAME] to stop and reflect, then I’ll make this a habit. - ung classmate [REDACTED_NAME] likod

Entry13196 Sorry if this feels DUGYOT, it’s my first time to do this and i want him to know what i feel. Okay, here’s the story. I never thought a simple online connection could make me feel this way again. I met this guy on the internet, and funny enough, we’re both from Region 4A, CALABARZON. But he’s currently studying in UB, a 2nd-year Nursing student. After class, he’s always at the gym, and every Sunday, he plays volleyball with his friends. We talked for only a couple of weeks, but the connection was so fast and so real. Every conversation felt valid — my feelings, my reactions, my thoughts. He wasn’t just about small talk; he was mature, deep, and genuine. One time, I told him, “I don’t think I’ll ever find someone like you.” And he answered, “Why would you still look for someone like me?” That line stayed with me. We played [REDACTED_NAME] together, and those midnight calls made me feel alive again. After two years of silence, I finally felt seen, heard, and appreciated. Even if it meant staying up late, those moments were some of the happiest I’ve had in a long time. We even planned to meet since our cities are close, but I got nervous. It would have been my first time, so I made excuses even though I really wanted to see him. Then came my “shut down” mode. For two weeks, I ignored him because that’s what I do when I’m not okay. But when I apologized, he still welcomed me back like nothing happened. That’s when I realized how kind he was. Later, I confessed that I liked him. At first, I thought it was only a casual fling because that’s what we both agreed on. But as time passed, my feelings grew real. One night, he confessed too. He said he liked me, but he didn’t want me to be dragged into his “messed-up life.” But all I could think was, “I don’t care. I like you.” Out of his long message, the words that stayed with me were, “I’m starting to like you.” And that was enough for me. Now our talks are on and off. He’s busy with summer classes and graveyard duties. It’s been two weeks since we last talked, and I really miss him. But even if we’re not talking, he became my inspiration — to lose weight, gain confidence, and improve myself. I don’t know if what he did was love bombing, but I really care and like him. And if he’s talking to or dating someone else now, I just hope he’s happy. I hope she makes him laugh the way he made me laugh. I hope she reminds him of his worth when he forgets it. Even if it hurts to know that it may not be me, it’s enough if he’s smiling. If destiny allows, I hope our connection continues. If not, maybe he came into my life only to remind me that love, hope, and happiness still exist. For now, I’ll just wait... even for the smallest sign. Thank you!!

Entry13168 Same Faces, Different Rooms [REDACTED_NAME]?? [REDACTED_NAME]? SHS Same Faces, Different Rooms Haii! It’s been a super long and tiring week. I really hope this gets fixed, especially for those who feel uneasy or scared because of past experiences. Entry13139 To the UB DENT PROFESSOR who publicly shamed a student in the hallway: It’s disheartening how a professor despite being respectfully asked to speak privately still chose to discuss a personal matter out loud, in front of other students. Why? Does public humiliation now pass as teaching? Does it feel rewarding to break someone down in front of their peers? You may say that students today are too sensitive but that’s not an excuse to be disrespectful. Sensitivity doesn’t equal weakness. It means students are more aware of their mental health, more conscious of what healthy environments should look like. And frankly, expecting basic respect isn’t too much to ask. How many more students have to go through this before someone listens? Normalizing public humiliation because it was “how things were” in past generations is not acceptable. We are not in the past. This is . And in this generation, we prioritize growth, safety, and mental wellness values that should be present in any educational institution. Respect is not a luxury; it’s the bare minimum. And no professor should ever feel entitled to strip someone of that in front of others. SECRET BASTA DENT

Entry13133 MY BULLY IS GRADUATED note: long post ahead please bare with me This graduation, I would just like to share an issue always overlooked by many. I am a 21 F, I am a victim of bullying. People might say, " why even post about it here on social media when you can approach proper outlets regarding your concern", well, i tried many times for months and the only thing i received was this " deal with the issue on your own, you are already in college and already a grown up" & was " [REDACTED_NAME] course tapos sa maliit na bagay hindi [REDACTED_NAME]". I can't even direct it towards higher authorities outside my department, if it is not processed or even addressed as an issue by the instructors & program chairs. I waited for a month with the hope that the issue will be addressed by 1 instructor, but that 1 month turned to a whole semester. I've kept it for a long time, a whole academic year staying quiet, tolerating the disrespect, the abuse, the manipulation, and belittling. I saw how the instructors I've reached out to, pass me by the hallways like I am nothing. I saw how they turned their backs and blinded their eyes. "[REDACTED_NAME], [REDACTED_NAME]", even outside campus the harr*ssment kept going. I saw how people g*slighted and turn things against me. " Kas*lanan mo bakit niya [REDACTED_NAME]". Even with all the proof, no justice was served. And during the last week of this academic year, i always pass by this posters posted within building A about anti-bullying advocacies & the Safe Space Act. Ironically, I always saw it hypocritical, because I witnessed how my bully and her group of friends advocated for it, one of them is even considered as a moderator for such seminars. Even in classes, many instructors would make insensitive comments and jokes regarding mental health as not a real problem to take one's *wn l*fe. " [REDACTED_NAME]?", " hindi [REDACTED_NAME] bakit pag nasabihan lang kayo ng ganto ganyan na ginagawa niyo hahaha", " baka sinasabi niyo [REDACTED_NAME] [REDACTED_NAME] trend" or " sinasabi [REDACTED_NAME] issues [REDACTED_NAME] sa psychiatrists". Not everyone one has the privilege to consult a psychiatrist, let alone having the strength to even approach one. " [REDACTED_NAME]* ka [REDACTED_NAME]" that word alone might seem very light to you and even joke about it often, but to someone, it can literally drive them to s**c*de because all you see is the person that they are within the campus, not the person they are outside, the environment they have in their homes, the problems that they are facing and the situation that they are in. School WAS a safe place for me to escape, but now it even drives me to give up. Anyways diba, I am just 1 student against 6 graduating bullies. It is 'okay' to lose 1 than losing 6. To my bullies, all i can say is that i hope life is better to you than it was for me, i hope it was worth all the effort of harrassing & abusing me. To my blind instructors, I hope life is also better to you and may you not be inf*cted with 'mental illness' virus. To me, [REDACTED_NAME] to [REDACTED_NAME]. To the admin please keep me anonymously even if the school demands it. -xiaoxiaoxiao

Entry13123 DND: Do Not Date (a Party Boy) Joining the Cheating Serye😆 Please keep my identity hidden please. I met this guy at a club — he was a friend of a friend. As time went by, we got closer and our bond deepened, but we never put a label on our relationship. We avoided the topic, and I thought we were on the same page about what we wanted. Every time we were together, his phone was on Do Not Disturb. I didn’t think much of it because I also use DND sometimes. I let him go out and party every weekend without fail, since he was a party boy anyway, and he told me he was always with friends — so I trusted him. That is, until I saw a photo he posted on his story — a group photo where he was holding a girl’s bag and standing right next to her. I asked him about it the next day, and he said his friend just asked him to hold it. It was the typical excuse, and I let it slide because I was already overwhelmed with my academic responsibilities. Months passed. We hung out with some of his friends, and he even introduced me to them — even if we still didn’t have a label. We started going to parties together too. One time, he randomly asked me when we were going to make things official. I was tipsy at the time, and I realized I had already fallen for him. I was just waiting for him to ask — so I said, "Let’s make it official then." He looked really shocked, and honestly, I didn’t know if he was happy or regretting that he even asked. One day, we went to a night market. After that, he said he needed to go home to freshen up, since he often slept at my apartment. We were on a call because he said he had to finish something and needed time. Suddenly, a message popped up on my screen — it was from a girl asking if I knew him. She said that she was in a relationship with him. Mind you, we had only been officially together for less than a week at that point. She said they were about to celebrate their one-year anniversary. I confronted him, and at first, he denied even knowing the girl. But when I showed him the receipts, he immediately ended the call, went straight to my apartment, and begged for forgiveness. He even cried — and believe it or not, he still tried to get intimate. The audacity of that man. Never ignore the red flags just because you’re hopeful they’ll turn green. No label doesn't mean no responsibility — if someone cares about you, they will make things clear. Trust is important, but so is protecting your peace. Don’t fall for the thrill of temporary excitement — especially with people who don’t show consistency or honesty. Being in a relationship with someone who respects, values, and chooses you every day is far better than chasing someone who only shows up on weekends. Girls, don't fall for party boys you meet in clubs. You deserve someone who brings peace, not paranoia. I'm now genuinely happy and in a healthy relationship with my boyfriend. [REDACTED_NAME]💖 Fyangerz [DEPARTMENT]

Entry13115 To the one who left while I was breaking, I was drowning. School was overwhelming, my family was a mess, and I had a competition that was slowly tearing me apart inside. I was holding on by a thread. I needed someone to lean on—but instead, I lost you. Yes, I got angry. I reacted to something you did that hurt me. I admit it—I shut down. I ignored you, not because I stopped loving you, but because I was trying to survive everything crashing down at once. I needed time. I needed space. I needed understanding. Instead, I got silence. And then, out of nowhere, you said we should break up. I didn’t even get the chance to explain. I was still trying to figure out how to fix things, still hoping we could find our way back—until I found out you had already replaced me. That hit me harder than anything. A month. Just one month. And suddenly you had someone new. Like I was that easy to forget. It made me question everything—was I really not enough? Was I that replaceable? Did any of it ever mean as much to you as it did to me? And the question that keeps me up at night: was it worth it? Was it worth throwing away everything we had for a year and a half—for someone new? Was I really that easy to leave? It wasn’t just a breakup—it was betrayal. I wasn’t perfect, but I was real. I loved you through all my chaos. And you gave up the moment I wasn’t easy to love. Still, thank you for walking away. Now I know who I am without you. I’m someone who feels deeply, who fights battles in silence, who’s learning how to stand up again. I still question do I deserved better? Ang heartbreak na hindi ako lilisanin —The one you gave up on

Entry13060 Academic Break “The Break That Never Came” (A Poem) She stares at the grade, a scarlet mark, A silent scream in the system’s dark. An engineer with trembling hands, Plans shattered like half-built strands. The world keeps turning, unaware, Of sleepless nights and hollow stares. Break has come—but not for her, Peace denied by a constant blur. “How can I rest?” her notebook weeps, “When failure stalks in quiet leaps?” A GPA, her worth defined— Each number like a rope, entwined. She whispers plans the void might hear, Of vanishing when no one’s near. But a voice inside, a softer breath: “Even steel bends—don’t choose death.” The weight is real. The storm is wild. But even now, you are still the child Who dreamed of circuits, stars, and skies— Don’t let that dream be where she dies. Dungeons of the HB..

Statement re: Lost and Found ID's Walang class? We hear you UBians! After our last post of a lost and found ID earned a high amount of engagement. Numerous people and trolls came forward about the dangers of the details not being censored. We edited the photo at tinira namin yung first names and photo of the individual but it was still called out not enough censorship. We apologize to the couple involved from the unnecessary comments they have gotten from the hit post as we tried to communicate using the page but it was not able to directly message the accounts. We tried to see if the people ONLY DIRECTLY INVOLVED with the post would message us or the post was something they just brushed off together as something irrelevant to their already strong relationship. We stand on protecting the privacy of the studentry and we will be censoring specific details regarding lost and found ID's, only leaving first initals or small details to avoid similar incidents like this. If your ID was posted on the page eversince the last 2 years we have been posting as recent admins without censorships, feel free to message us to avoid the risks of "Identity Theft" as you might be subject to being ja!led, scammed, st0len or whatsoever as claimed for the only recent post. We wouldn't want that would you? Please rely on the details of the entries sent by our senders to find your belongings. Thank you so much! -Admin Shadow

Entry13015 EXAM PAPERS I have nothing against paying for the exam papers, but I hope instructors can find a way to lower the price. Paying PHP 20+ for an exam paper that we only use for an hour or more is a bit heavy on our pockets. You could use brown paper(yung manipis), make the fonts smaller, use back-to-back questionnaires, or even remove the margins. I understand that the cost of the UB exam papers isn’t directly the fault of the instructors, but I hope we can find a way to make it more affordable for students. I know printing costs can add up, but if there are ways to minimize the costs without sacrificing quality or readability, that would really help a lot of us. AbunjingSa5thFloor

Entry12932 Hi Ms. [REDACTED_NAME], it's me again, the dentistry boy. I just wanted to tell you, my feelings for you are getting stronger every day. I can't keep them bottled up anymore. There's a pain pala that comes with liking someone who will never feel the same way no? kase every time I see you, it's like my heart tightens, reminding me of everything I'll never have. I stand there, watching you from a distance, and it hurts because I know that's all I'll ever do. I can't approach you, I can't be part of your life the way I want to. So, I just admire you from afar, because that's the only thing left for me to do I notice everything about you, even the smallest details that others might miss. The way your eyes sparkle when you smile, the way your voice softens when you talk to someone you care about. I see it all, and it only deepens the ache. I'm right there, close enough to know you, but too far away to ever be known by you. It's like I'm invisible, standing in the background, hoping for a moment that will never come. It's hard to accept that I'll never have a place in your world. You don't know it, but you're everywhere in mine. I replay our brief interactions in my head, wondering if you ever think about me, even for a second. But deep down, I know you don't. I'm just a face in the crowd, someone you pass by without a second thought, while every part of me is wrapped up in thoughts of you. It feels like a constant sting- knowing I'll never mean to you what you mean to me. There are moments when I imagine what it would be like to tell you how I feel, to let it all out. But I know it wouldn't change anything. You'd still look at me the same way you always do, with that friendly smile, never seeing me the way I see you. And I'd be left with nothing but the same aching emptiness I started with, only worse because now you'd know, and it still wouldn't matter. So, I keep my distance, pretending it's enough to just watch from afar. But the truth is, it's not. It's not enough, and it never will be. Admiring you from a distance is all I have, but it's also the thing that breaks me every time I see you. Because no matter how much I wish things could be different, I know they never will be. And that's what hurts the most. I watch you from a distance, knowing every glance steals a piece of my heart, but it's the closest I'll ever get. - BASTA SHE'S A MEDTECH. BEST OF LUCK MY FUTURE RMT.

Entry12886 "About Posts" Most of us are no longer minors. Please think wisely. I understand that many of you are irritated with someone or ranting about an issue that isn't ethical, or personal matter. Think critically before posting. Not everything needs to be shared, especially personal rants or vulgar language. And also can affect thousands of people and can spread negativity. Consider how your actions may be perceived as audacious and nonsensical to those who see your post. It's okay to express your feelings, but always consider whether your post is beneficial to yourself and others. I suggest sharing content that inspires, motivates, or shows concern or for our school. Entry12856 Let's stop stereotyping academic programs and accept that all programs have their own difficulties (mapa Crim, Engineering, Tourism man yan). We shouldn't belittle other programs just because ours is considered one of the hardest. Try putting yourselves in others' shoes; you might find their program isn't as easy as you think. It's easy to judge when we haven't experienced their hardships. Regarding the post that said "bb daw mga crim," some Criminology students' outrage is understandable. However, as future police officers or soldiers, your job is to maintain peace and order. The comments on that post show you're easily triggered (may nagmura pa ata). As future law enforcement, you need to learn to handle such issues; you should be putting out fires, not adding fuel. Remember, your job is to maintain peace and order. The post may have hurt, but as future professionals, you must learn to handle criticism. Consider this: one opinion is manageable; how will you handle many criticisms in the field? Will you react the same way, adding fuel to the fire, instead of finding a solution? Imagine a work scenario where you get into an argument that isn't your fault; if you react vi0l*ntly (because you can't control your emotions), you could lose your job. This applies to all professions, not just Criminology. Remember, your actions now will reflect on your future self. Ubian

Entry12801 Academic Burnout Hello this is my first time to rant sa ubf. And since grades are out for the 1st quarter ng 2nd semester for SHS, I would like to share my thoughts lang. My classmates knows me as someone who gives his best in everything and even considering me as their class top 1 kahit [REDACTED_NAME] class ranking. Theses past few weeks, after the exams, I was really overthinking about my scores pero turned out naman na I got the highest scores in most of my subjects. Because of this, my classmates always ask my scores everytime itu-turn back na yung exam papers and I hesitated to tell them cause I'm afraid na they'll compare my scores to their scores. I know for a fact that it is such an achievement to surpass your "the smartest in class" pero looking at it, I'm happy for them naman pero hindi [REDACTED_NAME] due to the fact na imbis they'll brag lang their score, ipinapamuka pa nila sa'kin na I got a lower score. When our subject teachers revealed our grades, I heard some of my classmates whisper "ang taas ni ***" not knowing [REDACTED_NAME] (pls change your perception na "ang taas mo na nga nagrereklamo ka pa) I'm grateful for the grade I got, but I really wished I had more than that. After my grade was revealed, my classmates will immediately ask me what my grade is and I didn't want to tell them because I'm afraid they'll use mine as a comparison to their scores again. [REDACTED_NAME] sasabihin nila sakin "ahy highest ka for sure" "ano 98?" while I shake my head as sign of disappointment and [REDACTED_NAME] nila sakin that I'm being TOO HUMBLE pero they don't know that they got higher grades than mine. [REDACTED_NAME] everytime may maglalabas ng grades I tend to tell them a line of 8 [REDACTED_NAME] I got line of 9 sa lahat [REDACTED_NAME]. Pero kahit hindi [REDACTED_NAME] they'll speak the same words "ikaw highest" "98 ka noh?" AND I WANT TO BREAK FREE FROM THESE SHACKLES OF ACADEMIC BURNOUT PLEASE LOWER DOWN YOUR EXPECTATIONS, I'M NOT AS PERFECT AS YOU THINK I AM. Some of you might tell me to just ignore them and study harder for myself, not for the others. BUT THAT'S THE COMPLETE OPPOSITE. I'M AFRAID TO DISAPPOINT THE PEOPLE AROUND ME ESPECIALLY FOR THOSE WHO HAVE EXPECTATIONS OF ME. Please, [REDACTED_NAME] ang senior life [REDACTED_NAME] if I have these burdens behind and on top of me? I lack sleep, I don't eat dinner with my family just to sit in front of my books and unfinished activities, I barely survived. So please, all I wanted is to break free from these. -humanistang gusto [REDACTED_NAME] boyfriend for life

Entry12761 Hi, I just wanted to share my experience at the UB clinic. I was really having a bad day, my stomach hurt so much that I could barely go down the stairs. The pain was unbearable, and I was sweating cold despite the discomfort. I couldn’t even eat, and to make things worse, that day was our practical exam. I decided to go to the clinic because I just couldn’t take it anymore. But when I got there, they simply told me it was just acid and gave me [REDACTED_NAME], even though I had already taken several medicines that didn’t work. After that, the pain only got worse, so intense that I had no choice but to rush myself to the ER at Notre Dame, even though it was raining and I was struggling just to move. When I arrived, it was already an emergency. They immediately put me on dextrose and started the necessary treatments. And as if things couldn’t get any harder, the next day was our exam. Despite being confined and hooked up to dextrose, my professor still had the nerve to tell me, “Come to class and take the exam.” Imagine being so weak, lying in a hospital bed with a n**dle in your arm, and still being pressured to show up for an exam it felt so unfair and heartless. Anonymous

Entry12747 whoever the sender is I believe we shouldn't dismiss other courses in the same way. Not all criminology students are unintelligent or uninterested in going abroad. The same can be said for forensic science, where many students are level-headed and motivated. Sometimes, there are unmet expectations, but this does not apply to everyone. I also think it’s important to recognize that everyone has their own motivations, destination, or course of action—just not immediately recognizable!It is also simply not acceptable to devalue criminology or any other course because of the course that you have taken. Each course has its own challenges and weaknesses, and every student works hard in their own way. Instead of commenting on the merit of another course of study we should support each other, regardless of one another’s journey.Every course serves its own purpose and value to society. Just because it does not resonate with you does not make it irrelevant. We need to acknowledge that everyone has their own strengths and passions. Rather than commenting on the merits of other's studies, we need to appreciate how every area serves as part of the larger whole. - not from scjps department

Entry12718 To that 4th year CE student, You were truly the unexpected person in my . We met back in December and decided to, what we called, 'date' last January. You never let me explore Baguio alone; we ate in different places, and we were both undeniably happy together. We celebrated the small achievements in our lives: mandatory lunches or dinners after exams, trying new things around town, and, most importantly, never worrying about the money we spent. You met my friends, we drank with your friends, we had lunch with my family for my birthday, and you were the one taking photos of my mom when we went out. We were just two people, happily spending time together, talking about our future, and me wiping away your tears when you missed your grandmother. Days, weeks, months passed, and I never thought this would all be possible until we reached January . Everything changed in a snap. You stopped calling, let me walk home alone after lunch, didn't talk to me for days, even weeks, hid your contacts from me while having beers with your friends, and slept at their places without even asking how I was or if I was even still al*ve. You left me without any reasonable explanation. You let my tears burst and insisted that I shouldn't feel this way because others have heavier problems than mine, invalidating my feelings. I was there when you needed me most, when you were crying in the middle of the night, but when I told you I was hurt by you, you just said you wanted to focus on yourself first. Suddenly, I became a stranger to you. All I wanted was for us to talk about everything. You know that I will always choose to understand and love you for who you are. I'm still waiting for you to realize everything. Pero ang [REDACTED_NAME] walang label. And yes, [REDACTED_NAME] label. -ur student pharm from the neighborhood

Entry12716 An Open Letter to the SBAA Student Body Council Dear SBAA Student Body Council, We hope this letter finds you well. We extend our gratitude for your unwavering commitment to representing and advocating for the student body. Your efforts in upholding the values and identity of our school are truly appreciated. Now, we write to you to humbly request an open dialogue regarding the SBAA Formal Corporate Uniform. As students, we acknowledge the importance of professionalism and unity that the uniform represents. However, we also believe that it is essential to ensure that the uniform aligns with the needs, concerns, and comfort of the students who wear it. In this regard, we respectfully urge the SBAA Student Body Council to initiate a formal consultation process to gather feedback and insights from the student body. This could be facilitated through surveys, open forums, or focus group discussions that will provide a comprehensive understanding of the students' perspectives. By fostering an inclusive and transparent discussion, we can work together towards a solution that considers the diverse opinions of the student community. We trust that the SBAA Student Body Council, as the voice of the students, will take this matter into serious consideration and take the necessary steps to address these concerns. Open communication and collaboration will undoubtedly strengthen our academic community and ensure that policies are fair, inclusive, and beneficial for all. We look forward to your immediate action and hope that you will advocate for the best interests of the students you represent. Thank you for your time, dedication, and commitment to serving the SBAA student body. -Concern SBAA Student

Entry12605 Musta ka?/ How are [REDACTED_NAME] ngy?/ I'm writing to remind us all to check our friends, classmates, and family. To Say "Musta ka na pre?”—it's more than a greeting it's a sign that you care. This afternoon a tragedy befell our section. We lost one of our dear classmates, and we were left in shock, sorrow, and immense pain. It was as if we lost a part of our classroom. He was the center of our section(noisiest section)—a section full of laughter, biruan, shouting, and joy. Let me start with the first time a met him and it was around the first week of this s.y he appeared to be a bad guy, perhaps even a bully. But I soon found out that he was very funny with infinite jokes.He may have appeared to be a typical boys at the back , but he was diligent and always finished his projects in time. He may not have been the brightest, but he was committed. This loss is particularly difficult since it was so very unexpected especially when losing a person with only eight school days remaining—it's nearly impossible to believe. One of my classmates send a screen shot at our gc and i thought “susss joke [REDACTED_NAME] impossible” the reality hit me hard the moment i search for his fb account where lots of post about his d**th popped up. This loss makes us remember to reach out to our classmates and friends, even those with their brightest smiles. We never really know what's going on within a person. It breaks our hearts to realize that we were laughing with him yesterday and today he is no longer with us. Let's hold our friendships dear and take care of one another, every single day. Let's enjoy the time we still have. To you pare [REDACTED_NAME] langit na ngayon We would like to say to you a very very very sad with a very very very very greatful heart-THANK YOU [REDACTED_NAME] [REDACTED_NAME], unforgettable memories and the friendship na galing sayo We thank God for the opportunity that we have been your brothers and sisters for a short time Psalm 34:18 The Lord is near to the brokenhearted and saves the crushed in spirit Pa post daw po 🖤

Entry12570 MENTAL HEALTH OF A STUDENT NURSE Shaken, exhausted, and constantly in a state of fight or flight. We’ve always been told that nursing is a demanding and mentally challenging profession. While that reality is something we accept, what’s difficult to understand is why some create an environment that feels more intimidating than educational—where students feel afraid and belittled. Instead of fostering learning, these interactions often leave us on edge, more focused on avoiding mistakes out of fear rather than growing from them. We understand that the profession comes with challenges far greater than what we experience now. However, is it truly so difficult to lead with kindness? Some manage to maintain high standards while still creating a space where students feel supported and encouraged to improve—why can’t others? Should submission be a reflection of fear rather than genuine admiration? While we have no control over how someone chooses to lead, I can only hope that as individuals, we strive for growth—not just in knowledge but in how we treat others. The world is already tough enough. Kindness costs nothing, and yet it has the power to make all the difference. 25

Entry12432 This is just a reminder for young peeps out there! I do hope that people still have the self-respect to avoid getting to the point where they end up putting themselves in a scenario that may dam*ge someone's relationship, even though the world of relationships is becoming frightening and chaotic. I continue to believe that stories of love began with a pure spark and sincere affection. However, someone who does not have self-respect and insist on fl*rting at the price of someone else's relationship. You wouldn't want to experience that kind of cruelty yourself, would you? That will undoubtedly bother you for the rest of your life. For those with partners, your journey is a true test of your foundation. How you two react to these encounters is entirely up to you. Ultimately, though, we have the freedom to decide whether or not to stay faithful. I still hope, though, that you have the self-respect to avoid becoming entangled in anything that will ruin what you've already started. It has an immessurable effect, particularly on your partner's overall state. Stay away from anything that you know may tempt you. Believe me, you are always aware of the consequences of your action. For the friends who put up with such behaviour. It reveals your identity. That remains how people remember you. I sincerely hope you take note of those and avoid doing so in the future. If you are in that person's position, you are undoubtedly aware of how difficult that is to absorb. Once more, I sincerely hope that you would stand up for what is right and have the guts to put an end to such horrible behaviour if you come across it in the future. To those true friends who, no matter the length of our friendship, found the bravery to communicate. I appreciate your willingness to speak up and acknowledge these codes. These codes may appear simple, requiring only basic common sense, which many lack. I am grateful for your insight. Your actions will certainly be remembered, and will always be thankful for it. -stela

Entry12375 UBHS Intramurals A.Y. '24-'25 i'm disappointed and frustrated with how our intramural schedules are being handled. The tryout announcements keep changing almost every day, and some students don’t even receive the same information because their adviser is too busy focusing on their assigned team or category, or they don't receive an update on time. Unlike before, we don’t even have an infographic on the Facebook page showcasing the official schedule for our different skills and academic events. None of us expected the intramurals to be this disorganized, and in some venues, there isn’t even a single facilitator present. So what’s the point of holding tryouts then, hm? And what about how our theme is being handled—especially for props and the mascot? We don’t even know the guidelines for making the props for the mascot, hip-hop costume, cheer props, and backdrop. (Correct me if I’m wrong about the prop groups, but this is what we got for our team.) That’s not all—how are we supposed to handle our budget? For the other groups, do you really expect only THREE leaders to train a group of more than 80 students in a small room for cheering? And what about other categories like hip-hop and aero? What happens to them if they don’t even have enough members to begin with? Why are there so many categories in this year’s intramurals compared to last school year, when most of us are still new to this? It's been almost 3 years since we had cheering from the bleachers and even so for props included in the high school's intrams. Now, a lot of students are leaving and joining other groups in their teams because they’re overwhelmed with the amount of tasks—just two weeks before our exams and performance tasks. It's unfair naman for us. I won't be surprised during the final intrams day that not everyone got to finish practicing on time. I get it naman when even the teachers are stressed, but please do so while remaining considerate to the rest of the student body and how much they can handle. Last intramurals was a success, so what happened now? :,( -ipis sa junior high school

Entry12374 What is up with the Legacy Building Elevator? I was at the 6th basement today, and I had a lot of stuff to carry. When I was done, I decided to take the elevator to the ground floor, so I pressed the button, and waited. I watched as the screen showed me the status of the elevators. One of them was at the 6th floor, and then the weight indicator turned on, so I knew people got on the elevator. It moved down to the 3rd floor, and then the ground floor, and then the weight indicator turned off (people got off the elevator), and then turned on again shortly after. The screen then showed that the elevator went up from the ground floor. What about me though? Didn't I call for the elevator at the 6th basement? I was left with no choice. I carried all of my stuff all the way up to the ground floor—12 flights of stairs, with about 10 kilograms of my gear. Unlike elevators in hotels, where you can call it to any floor, and go to any floor you like from there; I was under the assumption that the elevator would only go all the way up, and all the way down (as it's supposed to), and open its doors on the way where people ask for it to. If you were meaning to go up, then you only get on the elevator on its way up, and the same would go if it were the other way around. I wish they'd make it that way, so that everyone gets a fair chance to use to the elevator without having to wait for so long. SirNaMadalasPagkamalangMaam

Entry12360 INTRAMS NA NAKAKASTRESS AT DEPR*SS 😢 I want to speak up not just for myself but also for the students who participated in bench cheering, hip-hop, aero, and other events for the intrams in SHS and JHS. A week ago or a two, we were suddenly informed that we would have intramural practices, with no prior notice. To make matters worse, these practices were scheduled alongside our academic responsibilities. Our section had to rush through numerous school tasks while also trying to focus on practice sessions. Our minds are overwhelmed with schoolwork and rehearsals, leaving us with no time for ourselves, not even to rest. It feels unfair that we were only given 6 days to prepare for everything, and UB expects us to be completely ready and polished. On top of that, teachers were removed from the teams due to "competitive" concerns, leaving us without proper guidance. Many of us are new to this, and with just 6 days to prepare, it's hard to give a proper performance. In bench cheering, there were also a lot of misunderstandings. Initially, we all thought that the theme provided was already our team name. There was no clear theme given early on, and the guidelines stated that "stomping" was not allowed. After spending days practicing the cheers and moves without stomping, and already planning our costumes and props, we were suddenly informed on the 3rd or 4th day of practice that the theme was "Arcane" and "stomping" was now permitted. At this point, we had already planned everything, including our moves for the cheers, and were forced to change most of it due to this late announcement. To add, we need a total of at least 112+ members, we we're told that practices and sport tryouts/eliminations are going to be held in the same week. We had a hard time finding members to join in bench cheering not only bench but also aero. We had students coming in and out of the event just for tryouts, in general the whole week is super unorganized. With people losing in tryouts, new members will join in cheering and we have to teach them AGAIN from the very start. Our practice schedule was set from February 17-20, and again on February 24 and 26, with half days dedicated to academics (8:00-12:50, depending on the schedule) and the other half to practice (2:00-4:30 ONLY) . In total of 6 days, 2 hours and 30 mins per day which is basically 57.6 hours of practice ONLY. After that, we go back to regular classes followed by exam week and performance week. We’ll then have just two more practice days before the performance. Given this tight schedule, how can we effectively remember all our routines when we’re constantly being rushed? Why does it feel like finishing practices has become more important than academics? Why couldn’t we focus on our academics first, and then transition to intrams? As young students, we’re already feeling drained. I hope that UB understands the state that we are in and at least fix the given schedules as well as providing us an extension. ☹️ And yes, I know some people would say "time management". But if you were put into our situation with 6 days, 2 hours and 30 mins per day of practices. Let me ask you if you could finish a whole bench cheering routine, teaching about 90 students minimum. Including memorization of cheers and movements, as well as doing these while doing about several school works including the quizzes, mini tasks, written works, and learning new topics. IT IS NOT EASY. If you were given this short amount of time to prepare a huge performance while doing academics prioritizing your grades, you would understand the situation we are in and the immense pressure that we are going through. - mga pagod na istudyante sa jhs at shs

Entry12347 To the person behind Entry12325, Before throwing accusations, let’s stick to the facts. The Student Assistant (SA) you’re calling out never used a BOOK to ring the bell - let’s not spread false claims just to push a narrative. If you found the bell too loud, that’s understandable, but att*cking her personally (and even bringing up appearances) doesn’t make your point valid. She was simply following protocol as instructed by the head. That’s called doing her job, not power-tripping. Respecting library rules isn’t an act of superiority; it’s a responsibility. Calling out noise in a study area isn’t about power-tripping - it’s about ensuring fairness for those who actually want to focus. Rolling eyes? Honestly, if someone repeatedly ignores library rules and disrupts those who are actually want to study, anyone would be frustrated. That’s not being “[REDACTED_NAME],” just because she's an SA. There’s a huge difference between having authority and having an attitude - don’t mix up the two just because you don’t like being corrected. She’s doing what’s expected of her, not walking around with an inflated ego. Now, about that insult - calling someone’s attitude “[REDACTED_NAME] mo ugali mo"? only weakens your argument. It speaks more about you than the person you’re attacking. That’s not a clapback, that’s just plain pathetic. If the only way you can argue is by throwing cheap shots, then maybe the problem isn’t her attitude, but your inability to handle being corrected. If we’re really about “raising awareness,” Maybe, JUST MAYBE - reflect on why the bell had to be rung in the first place instead of ranting about it online. If you truly have concerns, instead of making personal attacks, it’s better to raise concerns properly through feedback to the library administration. I’m sure they’d be more than willing to listen. - 🤎

Entry12311 IMAGINE... You took a deep breath and sent a message to your professor, hoping to clarify a topic that had been swirling in your head. But instead of getting a helpful answer, you were met with a response that felt like a slap in the face: "I think I mentioned this one already during our class?" You felt a sting of embarrassment and frustration. Hadn't you been paying attention in class? Was your question really that silly? You couldn't shake off the feeling that your professor was annoyed with you. As a student, you expect your professors to be guides, mentors, and supporters. But in that moment, you felt like you were just an inconvenience. You wondered if you were the only one who felt this way. You know that professors are human beings too, with their own struggles and bad days. But as educators, they have a responsibility to create a safe and supportive learning environment. You're not asking for much, just a willingness to listen and answer questions with kindness and empathy. You hope that in the future, your professor will take the time to understand where you're coming from and provide the support and guidance you need to succeed. You're not just a student; you're a person with your own strengths, weaknesses, and struggles. A little kindness and compassion can go a long way. ps. NOT A MAJOR SUBJECT!! secret secret

Entry12112 Going up or down??? Why do people go inside the elevators if the indicator says it's going down yet the people are headed to the upper floors??? You could wait until the elevators are actually going up instead of going in right away even if it's headed to the lower floors. When it's headed down, you could at least wait until it's going back up because there's people waiting at the other levels of the building too, but they don't get to access the elevators since other people want it to go back up even if the elevator hasn't reached the bottom floors yet. Same thing if it's the other way around. Whenever I'm at one of the basement levels waiting for the elevators, sometimes it says it's already at let's say B3 but then it suddenly goes back up without going all the way down to the last basement, then I'd have to wait again. I take up less time using the stairs instead of waiting for the elevator😕 Pls gusto ko din gamitin yung elevator guys

Entry11822 - happy new year With many cheating incidents being exposed lately, I want to remind everyone, especially girls, to be mindful of their actions regarding someone else's partner. If you know that someone's partner feels uncomfortable with your presence, it’s important to distance yourself from them or rather than take the time for their partners to trust you. Let me share a short story. My girlfriend is very friendly, and I understand that she needs support with her studies. Recently, a new classmate started making flirty jokes with her. They would often unsend and delete their conversations, which feels inappropriate. This girl has normalized flirting with my partner and would talk sh*ts about me, as if she knows us so well. Ultimately, I broke up with my girlfriend because she chose to prioritize that friendship over our relationship. It was clear that the new friend was delighted to have my girlfriend's attention. Here comes my ex, who opened up about things we did in our relationship that were supposed to be private. She couldn’t keep it to herself because, in her story, this girl was pressuring her about whether we ever had any private sexual matters. To that girl, I hope you’re happy knowing that you played a role in ruining our three-year relationship, making yourself a homewrecker. My girlfriend started calling me stupid and immature and I believe she picked up from you. Your opinions about me are being reflected in what she says. Moving forward, I feel great and will never use that trauma as a reason to avoid loving someone again. To the ex of mine, I have no regrets at all, [REDACTED_NAME] [REDACTED_NAME] ikaw [REDACTED_NAME] As we move into , I hope that every girl can find peace and not spend their nights crying over relationships that bring them pain. It's crucial to recognize when to distance oneself and not let overthinking consume them. I hope the tables turn in favor of those who deserve better. Entry11709 Things that successful UBians always do No more complaining. Complaining doesn't fix things. Instead, focus on finding solutions and making things better. Stop comparing. Comparing yourself with others is one of the highest forms of self-destruction and people who love themselves do not destroy themselves. Skip the blame game. Do not blame your Teachers and classmates when you fail. Taking responsibility for your actions is key on how you learn and grow even when things get tough. Stay calm. Skip the arguments. Arguments rarely lead to good stuff. Stay calm and take a common ground instead. Stay humble. Skip the bragging. You don't need to show off. Your actions speak volumes and being humble earns respect. Calm conversations. No yelling. Yelling or getting angry to others doesn't solve problems. Speak calmly to be heard and understood. Learn to listen. Talk later. Successful people listen more than they talk. Understanding comes before sharing your thoughts. Be kind. Skip the judging. Instead of judging, put yourself in other's shoes. It builds empathy and better relationships. Stick to the truth. Skip to the lies. Honesty builds trust even when things get tough, it's the best way to go. The Game changer - I am pro-mental health

It was revealed that the reason why he transferred is because there were enough instances, it reached a point where he has to leave that school. Besides him, a first year student last school year (now second) was exposed of looking for $3x partners online, with his target age being 14-40(?) year-olds. Basically, this dude is a ped*ph*le given that he is an adult and is even older than most (if not all) of his classmates, despite that I still see him around campus. There are also stories I used to read here (in UB Files) that are similar to everything I have mentioned. Entry11554 Dear Ub Highschool Im an Shs Student 🤎🤎 Please [REDACTED_NAME], Extend the 2nd grading till next year January 🕯️ As everyone has been noticing we have been experiencing multiple typhoons which has led us to cancellations of classes. As of today, this is the third week of the second grading and we have had 5 days of classes in total. We have 3 weeks left before exams and there is one more typhoon to come. Less than 3 weeks to teach and to do projects and activites for something to be taught for 2- 3 months. How? Come on ub, extending the 2nd quarter till next year January isn't unprecedented. Its been done in Baguio National High school and others. We want to enjoy our lessons and projects. As a stem student I want to experience the experiments we are supposed to do and we paid for. We want to look at our microscopes, work in our tech labs, and experience our high school life with all its challenges. Additionally, [REDACTED_NAME], its 2 weeks from now and we've met with entrep 2-3 times, we are starting a business, common lets be realistic. Please [REDACTED_NAME] Let us have informative lessons and lectures [REDACTED_NAME] forward na to the point di na naituturo ang subjects. We want to learn, but we also want to have fun in classes [REDACTED_NAME] there's no rush. Lessen our stress increase our enjoyment! [REDACTED_NAME] give us the time to enjoy our education. I know there's a schedule but was the typhoons on it? The world is changing, its time to change with it.

The aim of this letter is to not invalidate the feelings of those students who have not attended the event with a valid reason. Oo, [REDACTED_NAME] tayo financially prepared [REDACTED_NAME] paggagastusan. Oo, mahirap [REDACTED_NAME] time to attend the event sa exam schedule. Anyways, refrain from submitting ourselves into suffocating situations na guys. Spread love and Padayon Natsci!

Entry11483 Inconsideration (and elitism) at its peak. I recently came across a post discussing the requirements imposed by certain professors for their students to attend the SNS Day and Night event, along with the allocation of extra credit for attendance. It raises significant concerns regarding fairness, especially for those who cannot attend due to pressing academic obligations, such as midterm exams. Many students juggle various responsibilities, and prioritizing our studies is crucial during this demanding period. I get the point of these kind of events but I really hope those certain professors would reconsider their decisions and have diverse perspectives, especially in terms of the challenges faced by students when establishing an attendance criteria for non-academic related events. And it’s such a degenerate move to gaslight your co-students just so you could socialize because you seem grown tired of your current friends (if you have one). But I guess some (lowkey elitist) ubians would really wake up just to k*ss an inconsiderate prof’s ass and call it a day, [REDACTED_NAME]? Entry11400 High Standard? I find it hard to agree with one of UB's objectives: to "exemplify a higher standard of learning." While the goal is admirable, the reality feels different. The 70% passing mark is tough, and quizzes are packed into single days. All majors seem to compete to see who can give the hardest quizzes, and the quality of teaching often leaves much to be desired. UB claims to uphold this high standard, but the actual learning experience doesn’t meet our expectations and needs. Our CIs are aware of how tough their subjects are, but they just present slides without really explaining them. That's why we end up going home without learning much, forced to self-teach and stay up late studying. We’re paying a lot in tuition, yet what we get in return doesn’t seem worth it. The semester is almost over, and we haven't even had lab activities, even though we pay high lab fees. The grading system sets high standards, but the teaching quality doesn’t match up. I’m a hardworking student, studying all night, but I still can’t meet these standards. Maybe it’s because all the subjects give quizzes daily, and the quizzes weren't even covered in the lecture. After each quiz, I question whether I didn’t study hard enough. Teachers seem proud of the high number of students who fail their subjects, but that should reflect poorly on their teaching methods.Ma'am/Sir, isn’t it time to question why so many students are unable to pass your subject? Maybe I'm not meant for UB, maybe UB is only for geniuses. I'm just a hardworking, ordinary student struggling to meet these high standards.

Entry10832 SSG Experience I have ran for the SSG before, and I want to say to all students to please be kinder to the people running for the SSG. The SSG is a big commitment, and students who run give out so many sacrifices. Also remember that they are your fellow students, so don't keep on b*shing then and bring*ng them down. Yes, some of them may be bad and have done awful things but, let's also remember that they also have feelings and are human beings, just like us. If you do have things you want to say about them, to share awareness to other students, please do so in a more mature or nice manner. I've seen some people do it here, and it's not that hard to present a proper and mature argument/rant. Let's not just paint the candidates in a bad light, because they also have good traits in them. I have read so many rants here, and most of them are just real mean and mostly immature and insensitive. Although, yes, some of those claims may or may not be right, we can still be more polite in addressing our opinions and thoughts about someone, because some of the rants here are just so toxic and irritating at this point. Yes, I do think that these rants are also helpful in guiding and informing students to know as to who they should vote, but most of the rants are having a negative effect on the mental health of those who are running. I remember when I ran, the students weren't that toxic and we're more nicer in addressing their dislike towards and about candidates running. That why, the process of running was just: you sign up for candidacy, if you pass the screening then you campaign, then you vote, then you either win or lose, and then the school year continues on. I also don't remember having so much beef with other party members or students of the school. If there were any problems, it was just simple arguments but, in the end, it still gets fixed. To all candidates, also remember that all of you running, whether you are in the same party or not, are all students. Why are you the ones bring*ng each other down? I've heard so many things a party says about another, and most of them are not good. You guys should be the first people to help one another, becuase you all have a mutual understanding and experience of running for the SSG. You all had problems preparing campaign materials, formulating platforms, practicing for campaigning, and getting excused in classes to campiagn. So then, why is it that some of you think that you should sh*t on each other, because you're in different parties? If you don't want to get bashed by other students, then be the role models you're supposed to be, and don't flex your crab mentality and personality to everyone who will be voting for you. Since today is the final day for the voting, I hope that well all be open minded, and give a chance to those who win, because we never know just how capable they are to lead the school and become our officers. This is the end of my rant. I hope that we all have a great day and may all of us, and our families, stay safe. Always remember that you matter and that you are worth it 😊 [REDACTED_NAME] SHS

Entry10821 OPEN LETTER It's too disturbing to not say anything. As a student of the [CAR], it's a shame that these actions of other students affects those students who just wants to study, learn and finish their bachelors degree. These students who kept on complaining about the policies of UB and [REDACTED_NAME] that they are doing that to merely open the minds and eyes of those who are "blind" enough to just comply with the policies. First of all, these policies had no issues before you entered the University. Colleges are different from Elementary, Junior and Senior High School. My GREATEST LOVE without label, was five years ago and you're still as beautiful as the day I lost you. If only I was more mature then, then maybe, just maybe, we would've worked out. There are times where I want to, but it bothers me that if I take it, I would just make the same mistakes again... Honestly I don't want that to happen, I don't ever wanna see me make regrets that will weigh on my mind for years to come. I do not know if I'm matured enough right now to try again... It's been almost five years since our last conversation before any of this happened, before I reconnected to you again. But yeah, maybe this thing that I've been overthinking is not as important to you, maybe I was not that memorable to you, maybe I was just some guy you gave a chance and wasted it... Time moves forward, and so should I, all things in the past must remain in the past no matter how much I want to bring it back... But the world is not absolute. So my GREATEST LOVE without label, our chapter's closed, but the book's still not finished. You hold the pen in which may continue and bring back what once was. Entry10097 BE HUMAN for your sake Read at your own risk Calling the attention of all UBiano’s especially college students like me. We all know that AI such as Chatgpt, perplexity, CiCi, and other similar [REDACTED_NAME]’s Have become a great help to our assignments, projects, researches To the point that we have been relying on it everytime we get those activities, but let me tell you something, activities are there for you to develop your critical thinking skills, creativity and will shape you to become the best version of yourself. Don’t underestimate yourself, don’t let the Ai generation produce the output for you, try to make it, at least genuinely you can proudly say, “That is my creation” and I’m proud of it... These AI’s are creations of humans designed to help you in overcoming “writer’s block” or when you can’t properly formulate what’s on your mind in producing the output that you want. Please please for your sake don’t rely in Ai too much!! I beg you not to be a fool!! Because the last time I got into groupings and we are assigned to report on a particular subject, almost all of my groupmates gave me generated paragraphs coming from chatgpt!! Like wtf this is an important subject! And you give me trash paragraphs like what the heck is this. It’s super disappointing to know you have plenty of time to do it but here you are procrastinating and then sending Ai made outputs because of your laziness. P.s YOU ARE COLLEGE STUDENTS!! Prove that you are better than your Juniors!! GROW THE F*CK UP! GalitsaAi SCJPS 2ndyr

Entry PRACTICE VIGILANCE AT ALL TIMES - Hello, I am a transferee from Manila and currently a 3rd-year BSMLS student. Due to some prerequisite policies, I am taking subjects typically taken by 2nd-year students. This issue is not exclusive to BSMLS but affects all programs, and I want to specifically address it with the BSMLS Department. I have noticed a concerning trend among students during quizzes, seatworks, and other written assessments. Many students seem to take advantage of the "less strict quiz policies," leading to instances of cheating. I have observed students looking at their seatmates' papers, writing answers on blank sheets while checking, and even using their phones during quizzes. While I understand that it is not my place to call them out directly, it feels unfair to those who are genuinely studying and working hard to complete their assessments with integrity. To the UB Departments, especially those in allied health fields, could you please address this issue? Simple measures such as instructing professors to seat students one seat apart during exams or having professors monitor students more closely while they are taking tests could help reduce these problems. This way, we can minimize cheating, and those who disobey could be appropriately penalized. As a 3rd-year MLS student, this issue is particularly alarming. In our future roles as Registered Medical Technologists (RMTs), the accuracy of our results is crucial, as these results are used to diagnose patients and assist physicians. If students cheat on assessments now, it raises concerns about the integrity of their future work. I hope the university enforces its NO CHEATING POLICY seriously during class hours and assessments. The penalties for cheating are severe for those caught, but are these policies sufficient to ensure that students remain vigilant? Thank you. Lastly, to my fellow students: I understand that studying is challenging, but I hope you can appreciate the satisfaction of earning a high score through your own knowledge rather than resorting to cheating. Dealing with a condensada friend [REDACTED_NAME] is just the term I used to describe this so-called new friend of mine, so as to label the personality of his that I'm not quite fond of. There are times he'd make me feel small and stupid, times when I'd just ask something and he'd respond in a condescending manner and even include rude mannerisms like rolling eyes and giving me snide remarks and side-eyes, I don't know if he's even aware that I'm clearly bothered, I don't even give a smile nor a nod anymore whenever they do this (like I usually do). [REDACTED_NAME] here actually, so we've just been friends for at least a week and a few days but what I find weird is that he's already being like this (that's on me for assuming I already know him enough to expect him to not be like this, but common decency is already to be expected among others but ig not in this case). Just earlier last week (first week of classes) actually we were working on something, whenever I'd make my own part, he'd just micromanage, calling it false already without providing actual constructive criticism, just blatantly labelling everything wrong wrong wrong until its done his way, I can't even do my own part without it being revised to hell and back, and to be honest it's making me doubt my skills. I admit I'm not the sharpest person, more average actually, but the way he is with things like these it almost makes me feel like I'm just stupid, that I don't listen, ah their favorite line "Di ka nanaman nakinig sa Prof" after I just wanted to clarify something, it almost makes me not want to ask things anymore, even when I ask something to the Prof, they just start saying "Di mo [REDACTED_NAME]?". What is this person's deal? I don't get why they are like this towards me, I shouldn't really care like others would advise but unfortunately I do because I considered him as my friend and I wouldn't do this to them, never did, never will. Please just give advice, thank you. Have mercy on a freshie probably going through the "first year friendships dont last" situation. [REDACTED_NAME]

UB, HERE US OUT Dear Administration, I’m reaching out because a lot of students are really worried about the new payment policy, especially the "no validated id, no entry" rule. We know the school needs to manage its finances, but this policy is hitting most of us hard. Not everyone can afford to pay the full down payment upfront, and with everything going on, this just adds more stress. We’re all trying to keep up with our studies, and now some of us are scared we might not be able to continue because we can’t pay right away. We’re hoping you can listen to what we’re saying and consider making the policy more flexible. Maybe there’s a way to let us validate our IDs without needing to pay everything at once. It would really help ease a lot of the pressure we’re feeling. Education should be something everyone can access, no matter their financial situation. We hope you can understand where we’re coming from and consider making some changes so no one has to miss out because they’re struggling to pay. Student Ally SIHTM

"HM kami hindi HM LANG" PHILIPPINES IS ONE OF THE MOST VISITED COUNTRIES BECAUSE OF TOURIST SPOTS AND HOSPITALITY OF THE FILIPINO PEOPLE. Because of this, a lot of people are saying that they are "PROUD TO BE PINOY". But how ironic that the Bachelor of Science in Hospitality and Tourism Management Program in college was discriminated against by a lot of Filipinos? "[REDACTED_NAME] "hila-hila ng maleta" "[REDACTED_NAME]" "[REDACTED_NAME] bedsheet" Let's say that you choose a program that has a board exam, a program that is admired by a lot of people, and the most difficult to survive program. In the first place, you chose that path, and I chose this. So, you have no right to belittle someone's course or choice. BSHM and BSTM have never been easy. So that's how little you saw from the people who assist you when you enter a hotel, resort, or restaurant? If you want to relax and have a vacation, the people who will guide you to the destinations, the people at the front desk who have to smile all day just to assist you, The people who made a room make up in order for you to have a good room in a hotel, like a flight attendant who wear heels and smiles all the time for you to serve. The people who make your food when you order because you have no time to cook. The organizers behind your successful personal events! These are all in the HOSPITALITY and TOURISM industries. Come on, people, we're in the 21st century. Wake Up!!! It's time to recognize and respect the value of every profession. The hospitality and tourism industry is a vital part of our economy and society. Let’s wake up and give due respect to those who dedicate their lives to making ours more comfortable and enjoyable. HM KAMI, HINDI HM LANG TM , HINDI TM LANG IHTM
